title: NBA Daily Rundown: March 30, 2026
subtitle: Your daily dose of hoops action and breaking news!

items.headline: Warriors Eyeing Kawhi Leonard, LeBron James Amid Offseason Rumors
items.summary: The Golden State Warriors reportedly attempted to acquire Kawhi Leonard at the trade deadline and are expected to pursue him this offseason, potentially as a free agent if an ongoing NBA investigation into the Clippers' alleged salary cap circumvention voids his contract. Additionally, the Warriors are also rumored to be a 'credible destination' for LeBron James should he depart the Lakers.

items.headline: Boston Celtics Clinch Playoff Berth
items.summary: The Boston Celtics have officially secured a spot in the 2026 NBA Playoffs following their latest victory, solidifying their strong regular season campaign.

items.headline: Wizards Plagued by Injuries as Season Nears End
items.summary: The Washington Wizards continue to battle significant injury issues, with key players such as Trae Young (quadricep), Anthony Davis (finger), Kyshawn George (elbow), Cam Whitmore (shoulder - out for season), Alex Sarr (toe - day-to-day), and Tre Johnson (foot - day-to-day) all facing time on the sidelines.

items.headline: Blazers Top Wizards Despite Avdija's Effort
items.summary: The Portland Trail Blazers defeated the Washington Wizards, who were without several key players. Deni Avdija led the Wizards in scoring with 26 points.

items.headline: Clippers Extend Win Streak Over Short-Handed Bucks
items.summary: Bennedict Mathurin's 28 points powered the LA Clippers to their fifth consecutive win against a Milwaukee Bucks squad missing Giannis Antetokounmpo.

items.headline: Thunder Ride SGA's 30 Points Past Knicks
items.summary: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander dropped 30 points, extending his NBA record for consecutive 20+ point games to 135, as the Oklahoma City Thunder defeated the New York Knicks.

items.headline: Celtics Down Hornets to Clinch Playoff Spot
items.summary: The Boston Celtics secured a playoff berth with a decisive victory over the Charlotte Hornets.

items.headline: Jokic Leads Nuggets to Sixth Straight Win Over Warriors
items.summary: Nikola Jokic posted 25 points and 15 rebounds, just shy of a triple-double, as the Denver Nuggets extended their winning streak to six games against the short-handed Golden State Warriors.

items.headline: Nets Victorious Over Kings
items.summary: The Brooklyn Nets defeated the Sacramento Kings in a Sunday night matchup.

items.headline: Suns Dominate Jazz Behind Green's 31 Points
items.summary: Jaylen Green poured in 31 points to lead the Phoenix Suns to a convincing win over the Utah Jazz. Kyle Filipowski added 26 points and 9 rebounds for Utah.

items.headline: Pistons Defeat Timberwolves
items.summary: The Detroit Pistons secured a victory against the Minnesota Timberwolves.

items.headline: Spurs Rout Bucks with Wembanyama and Castle
items.summary: The San Antonio Spurs delivered a dominant performance against the Milwaukee Bucks, with Victor Wembanyama contributing 23 points and 15 rebounds, and rookie Stephon Castle recording a triple-double.

items.headline: Atlanta Hawks vs. Boston Celtics
Los Angeles Lakers vs. Washington Wizards
Miami Heat vs. Philadelphia 76ers
San Antonio Spurs vs. Chicago Bulls
Dallas Mavericks vs. Minnesota Timberwolves
Phoenix Suns vs. Memphis Grizzlies
Utah Jazz vs. Cleveland Cavaliers
Detroit Pistons vs. Oklahoma City Thunder
Shai Gilgeous-Alexander Extends 20-Point Streak
items.summary: OKC's Shai Gilgeous-Alexander dropped 30 points against the Knicks, extending his remarkable NBA record for consecutive games with at least 20 points to 135.

items.headline: Alperen Şengün's 36-Point Masterclass
items.summary: Alperen Şengün delivered a historic 36-point performance in a recent Houston Rockets game, turning heads around the league and bolstering the Rockets' playoff push.

items.headline: Jaylen Green Leads Suns with 31 Points
items.summary: Phoenix Suns guard Jaylen Green erupted for 31 points in the team's victory over the Utah Jazz.

items.headline: Rookie Stephon Castle Records Triple-Double
items.summary: San Antonio Spurs rookie Stephon Castle showcased his all-around game with a triple-double performance, tallying 22 points, 10 rebounds, and 10 assists against the Bucks.

items.headline: Deni Avdija Paces Wizards with 26 Points
items.summary: Israeli forward Deni Avdija led the Washington Wizards in scoring with an efficient 26 points, including 4-of-8 from three-point range, against the Portland Trail Blazers.

items.headline: Nikola Jokic Nears Another Triple-Double
items.summary: Denver's Nikola Jokic delivered another dominant performance with 25 points and 15 rebounds against the Warriors, falling just two assists shy of his fifth straight triple-double.

items.headline: Bennedict Mathurin Drops 28 for Clippers
items.summary: Bennedict Mathurin was a scoring force for the LA Clippers, putting up 28 points in their victory over the Milwaukee Bucks.

items.headline: Giannis Antetokounmpo Remains Sidelined for Bucks
items.summary: Milwaukee Bucks superstar Giannis Antetokounmpo missed his seventh consecutive game due to a left knee hyperextension and bone bruise.

items.headline: Stephen Curry Misses 25th Consecutive Game
items.summary: Golden State Warriors' Stephen Curry has now missed 25 straight games as he recovers from a right knee issue.

items.headline: Oklahoma City Thunder Maintain Top Spot in NBA Standings
items.summary: The Oklahoma City Thunder continue to lead the NBA standings, further solidifying their position as one of the league's elite teams this season.

items.headline: Knicks Eyeing Center Replacements Amid Offseason Questions
items.summary: With Mitchell Robinson heading into unrestricted free agency and potential trade rumors surrounding Karl-Anthony Towns, the New York Knicks are reportedly exploring options to add depth at the center position, including drafting Arizona's Motiejus Krivas.
